keys.description: When true, creating a new Organization auto-seeds a default Branch under it (the tenant's default region is used). Default false — branches are explicit tenant admin actions.
Initial status for newly-provisioned Branches. Default active. Set to paused for enterprise onboarding flows that pre-create branches during setup + require an explicit resume.

Global toggle. Off = every geocoding call short-circuits regardless of tenant entitlement. Overrides the per-tenant `branch_geocoding` entitlement. Used during subprocessor outages via the `branch.geocoding_enabled` feature flag.
The default geocoding subprocessor. Per-tenant override via `TenantSetting.branch.geocoding.provider`. The `null` driver is a no-op used in tests.
Confidence threshold below which the geocoded result is refused. Coordinates stay at their prior value; caller falls back to manual pin drop.
API key for the Google Maps Geocoding API.
Nominatim requires a contact email in the User-Agent header per their usage policy.
Nominatim's public tile is rate-limited to 1 req/s per the usage policy. Set to 0 to disable throttling when hitting a private Nominatim instance.
Default open time for weekday periods when the observer seeds initial opening_hours for a new Branch.
When true, seeded opening_hours mark Saturday + Sunday as closed. Default false — most sports venues operate weekends.
Global default for the `branch.enforce_operating_hours` middleware. Tenant setting may override.
Schedule string for ReconcileDefaultBranchInvariantJob. Interpreted by the shared scheduler service.
When true, `address` is required on Branch creation. Default false — allows sparse initial creation for tenants importing a legacy roster and backfilling addresses later.
Upper bound on the length of the amenities array. Defence in depth against a malicious client submitting a 10k-element array.

Registry of downstream table+column pairs that block closure. Modules opt in by appending to this list from their own service providers via `Config::push('branch.close.dependency_checks', ...)`. Empty entries are silently skipped — modules that haven't landed yet are safe by design.
